title: puya alpestris subsp. alpestris
intro: Puya alpestris subsp. alpestris, also known as the Andean puya, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Bromeliaceae family. It is native to the Andean region of South America and is used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant contains compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
